 {"id":"bd-1olr","title":"Document filter mode in TUI help overlay","description":"# Document Filter Mode in TUI Help

## Problem
The TUI has a filter mode (activated by /) that is completely undocumented. In widgets.rs line 109:
```rust
if state.filter_mode {
    render_filter_input(frame, state, &colors);
}
```

Users discover this by accident. The help overlay (?) doesn't mention it.

## Current Help Overlay Content
From render_help_overlay:
```
q/Esc   Quit
↑/↓     Navigate
Tab     Switch panel
r       Refresh
?       Toggle help
```

## Updated Help Content
```
Navigation
  ↑/↓ j/k   Navigate up/down
  Tab       Next panel
  Shift+Tab Previous panel
  1-4       Jump to panel

Actions
  Enter     Select/expand
  r         Refresh data
  c         Copy to clipboard
  d         Drain worker (Workers panel)
  e         Enable worker (Workers panel)
  k         Cancel build (Builds panel)

Search
  /         Open filter (Build History)
  Esc       Close filter
  Enter     Apply filter

General
  ?         Toggle this help
  q/Esc     Quit
```

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/tui/widgets.rs (render_help_overlay function)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:04:17.153326791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:19:20.892931011Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:19:20.892861572Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1olr","depends_on_id":"bd-ryot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T17:00:04.087874499Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":61,"issue_id":"bd-1olr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (rch/src/tui/widgets_test.rs)
1. `test_help_overlay_contains_filter_shortcut()` - Verify / is documented
2. `test_help_overlay_contains_all_shortcuts()` - Verify completeness
3. `test_help_overlay_section_ordering()` - Verify logical grouping
4. `test_help_overlay_fits_80x24()` - Verify renders in minimal terminal

### Integration Tests
1. Render help overlay and verify all keybindings are documented
2. Press each documented key and verify action occurs
3. Verify no undocumented keybindings exist

### E2E Test Script (scripts/test_tui_help.sh)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG_FILE=${RCH_TEST_LOG:-/tmp/rch_tui_help_test.log}

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }

log "Testing TUI help overlay completeness"

# Dump help overlay content
HELP_OUTPUT=$(rch dashboard --test-mode --show-help --dump-state 2>&1)

# Required shortcuts that must be documented
REQUIRED_SHORTCUTS=(
  "/" "filter" "search"
  "j" "k" "navigation" "up" "down"
  "d" "drain"
  "e" "enable"
  "q" "quit"
  "?" "help"
  "Tab" "panel"
)

for shortcut in "${REQUIRED_SHORTCUTS[@]}"; do
  if ! echo "$HELP_OUTPUT" | grep -qi "$shortcut"; then
    log "FAIL: Missing documentation for: $shortcut"
    exit 1
  fi
  log "PASS: Found documentation for: $shortcut"
done

log "All help overlay tests passed"
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log help overlay render at DEBUG level
- Log any keybinding conflicts at WARN level","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:55:35Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1q0e","title":"WS4.3: Add comprehensive tests for multi-command classification","description":"Comprehensive test suite for multi-command classification (WS4):

1. SPLIT_COMMANDS UNIT TESTS:
   - Single command: "cargo build" → ["cargo build"]
   - Double: "cargo fmt && cargo build" → ["cargo fmt", "cargo build"]
   - Triple: "cd /proj && cmake .. && make" → ["cd /proj", "cmake ..", "make"]
   - Or-chain: "make || echo fail" → ["make", "echo fail"]
   - Mixed: "cd /tmp && make -j4 || echo fail" → ["cd /tmp", "make -j4", "echo fail"]
   - Empty segments: " && cargo build" → ["", "cargo build"] or ["cargo build"]
   - No delimiters: "cargo build --release" → ["cargo build --release"]

2. QUOTING EDGE CASES:
   - Single quotes: "echo '&&' && cargo build" → ["echo '&&'", "cargo build"]
   - Double quotes: "echo \"&&\" && cargo build" → ["echo \"&&\"", "cargo build"]
   - Backticks: "echo \`a && b\` && cargo build" — handle or document limitation
   - Escaped quotes: "echo \'hello && cargo build" — tricky, document behavior
   - Nested: "echo \"it's && done\" && make" → ["echo \"it's && done\"", "make"]

3. CLASSIFICATION INTEGRATION TESTS:
   - "cargo fmt && cargo build" → is_compilation: true
   - "cd /project && make -j8" → is_compilation: true
   - "export RUSTFLAGS='-C opt-level=3' && cargo build --release" → is_compilation: true
   - "echo hello && echo world" → is_compilation: false
   - "ls -la && cat file.txt" → is_compilation: false
   - "git status && git log" → is_compilation: false
   - "mkdir -p build && cmake -B build && cmake --build build" → is_compilation: true

4. REGRESSION TESTS:
   - All existing classification tests must still pass
   - Single-command classification is unchanged
   - Performance: classify_command("cargo fmt && cargo build") completes in <5ms

Test location: rch-common/src/patterns.rs (#[cfg(test)] module)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:09.996516181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T23:26:50.705618592Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1q0e","depends_on_id":"bd-16t3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:26:50.705588416Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1q0e","depends_on_id":"bd-1qt9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:35.685662639Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1q0e","depends_on_id":"bd-9ka6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:10.003858969Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1q0e","title":"WS4.3: Add comprehensive tests for multi-command classification","description":"Comprehensive test suite for multi-command classification (WS4):

1. SPLIT_COMMANDS UNIT TESTS:

   Basic splitting:
   - "cargo build" → ["cargo build"]
   - "cargo fmt && cargo build" → ["cargo fmt", "cargo build"]
   - "cd /proj && cmake .. && make" → ["cd /proj", "cmake ..", "make"]
   - "make || echo fail" → ["make", "echo fail"]
   - "cd /tmp && make -j4 || echo fail" → ["cd /tmp", "make -j4", "echo fail"]
   - "a ; b ; c" → ["a", "b", "c"]
   - " && cargo build" → ["", "cargo build"] or ["cargo build"] (decide behavior)
   - "cargo build --release" → ["cargo build --release"]

2. QUOTING EDGE CASES:

   Single quotes:
   - "echo '&&' && cargo build" → ["echo '&&'", "cargo build"]
   - "echo 'hello;world' && make" → ["echo 'hello;world'", "make"]

   Double quotes:
   - "echo \"&&\" && cargo build" → ["echo \"&&\"", "cargo build"]
   - "echo \"it's && done\" && make" → ["echo \"it's && done\"", "make"]

   Backticks (legacy command substitution):
   - "echo \`date\` && cargo build" → ["echo \`date\`", "cargo build"]
   - Document if backtick-nested && is handled or not

   Escaped quotes:
   - "echo \\\"hello && cargo build" → behavior depends on shell, document choice

   ANSI-C quoting ($'...'):
   - "$'hello\\nworld' && cargo build" — document if supported

3. CLASSIFICATION INTEGRATION TESTS:

   Should classify as COMPILATION:
   - "cargo fmt && cargo build" → is_compilation: true
   - "cd /project && make -j8" → is_compilation: true
   - "export RUSTFLAGS='-C opt-level=3' && cargo build --release" → is_compilation: true
   - "mkdir -p build && cmake -B build && cmake --build build" → is_compilation: true
   - "echo 'Starting...' && cargo test" → is_compilation: true (second segment)

   Should classify as NON-COMPILATION:
   - "echo hello && echo world" → is_compilation: false
   - "ls -la && cat file.txt" → is_compilation: false
   - "git status && git log" → is_compilation: false

4. FAIL-OPEN EDGE CASES:

   Malformed input (should NOT panic, return usable result):
   - Unclosed single quote: "echo 'hello && cargo build"
   - Unclosed double quote: "echo \"hello && cargo build"
   - Unclosed backtick: "echo \`date && cargo build"
   - Empty string: ""
   - Only whitespace: "   "
   - Very long input (10KB+): verify no timeout/panic
   - Input with null bytes: "cargo\x00build" (if applicable)
   - Unicode: "echo '你好' && cargo build"

5. REGRESSION TESTS:
   - All existing classification tests must still pass
   - Single-command classification behavior unchanged
   - Performance: classify_command("cargo fmt && cargo build") completes in <5ms

6. PIPE PRESERVATION:
   - "cargo build 2>&1 | tee log" → single segment (pipe is NOT a command separator)
   - "make 2>&1 | grep error && echo done" → ["make 2>&1 | grep error", "echo done"]

Test location: rch-common/src/patterns.rs (#[cfg(test)] module)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:09.996516181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T23:47:58.373012741Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1q0e","depends_on_id":"bd-16t3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:26:50.705588416Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1q0e","depends_on_id":"bd-1qt9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:35.685662639Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1q0e","depends_on_id":"bd-9ka6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:10.003858969Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1q3p","title":"Idea: Soft-fail artifact retrieval with actionable diff","description":"## Background
Artifact retrieval can fail even if remote build succeeded (rsync issues, permissions). Hard failure can confuse users if build output already streamed. A softer error with actionable detail improves UX.

## Goals
- Detect artifact retrieval failures and present clear remediation.
- Keep fail-open semantics for subsequent commands.

## Design / Approach
- On artifact retrieval failure, log which patterns were attempted and rsync stderr snippet.
- Return a warning and allow continuation (no denial change for already executed commands).
- Provide `rch diagnose` suggestion to re-run with verbose or fetch artifacts manually.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Enhance error classification for retrieve_artifacts.
- Add warning display + JSON fields for automation.
- Update docs/troubleshooting.

## Tests
- Unit: error classification includes attempted patterns.
- Integration: mock retrieval failure yields warning (not panic).
- E2E: warning displayed in verbose mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Retrieval failure is reported clearly without crashing.
- User sees actionable next steps.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-1q3p.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Remote success but artifact retrieval fails -> warn but keep deny.
- Include attempted patterns + rsync stderr snippet.
- Provide actionable follow-up steps in message.

## E2E Outline
- Mock retrieval failure -> warning emitted with patterns.
- Exit code unchanged from remote success path.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/transfer.rs: artifact retrieval failure classification.
- rch/src/hook.rs: warning path does not alter deny/allow semantics.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-1q3p.sh: mock retrieval failure -> warning + actionable info.
","notes":"## Implementation Complete (EmeraldMoose)

### Changes Made:
1. **Added ArtifactRetrievalWarning struct** (rch/src/error.rs)
   - Captures attempted patterns, rsync stderr snippet, exit code
   - Includes actionable suggestions
   - Provides format_warning() for human-readable output
   - Provides to_json() for machine-readable output
   - Implements Display trait

2. **Updated artifact retrieval error handling** (rch/src/hook.rs)
   - Extracts rsync exit code from error message
   - Creates structured ArtifactRetrievalWarning on failure
   - Shows detailed warning in verbose/interactive mode
   - Outputs JSON in machine mode for automation
   - Maintains soft-fail semantics (compilation still succeeds)

3. **Added 6 unit tests** for ArtifactRetrievalWarning:
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_basic
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_format
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_json
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_truncates_stderr
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_with_custom_suggestions
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_display_trait

### Remaining work:
- E2E test script (scripts/e2e_bd-1q3p.sh) - optional
- Update docs/troubleshooting - optional

### Verification:
- cargo check passes
- All 6 new tests pass
- 1099 total tests in rch package pass","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T22:55:32.592525740Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T05:12:05.635332867Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T05:12:05.635214798Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1qfm","title":"Add code coverage reporting (cargo-tarpaulin or llvm-cov)","description":"**Add code coverage reporting with cargo-tarpaulin or llvm-cov**

## Scope
Integrate code coverage tooling into the CI pipeline:
- Choose between cargo-tarpaulin (easier) or llvm-cov (more accurate)
- Generate coverage reports in multiple formats (HTML, LCOV, JSON)
- Upload to coverage service (Codecov or Coveralls)

## Requirements
- Coverage for all crates (rch, rchd, rch-common, rch-wkr, rch-telemetry)
- Exclude test code from coverage metrics
- Generate per-file and per-function coverage
- Integration with GitHub Actions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Coverage reports generated on every CI run
- [ ] HTML report viewable as artifact
- [ ] Coverage badge in README
- [ ] Per-crate coverage breakdown
- [ ] Historical coverage tracking","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteDune","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:03:31.044005621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:10.481769932Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:10.481689001Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria verified: CI generates LCOV/HTML reports, Codecov badge in README, per-crate summary via coverage-summary.md, test code excluded via .cargo/config.toml","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qfm","depends_on_id":"bd-2mc4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:05:05.304381387Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1qt9","title":"WS4.2: Integrate sub-command classification into classify_command()","description":"Modify the classification flow to handle multi-command strings:

Option A (recommended): Modify classify_command() at line 243:
  1. Call split_shell_commands(cmd)
  2. If only 1 segment → proceed as before (no behavior change)
  3. If multiple segments → classify each through the full pipeline
  4. Return the "most significant" result:
     - If ANY segment is compilation → return compilation (with highest confidence)
     - If ALL segments are non-compilation → return non-compilation

Option B: Modify check_structure() to skip &&/||/; rejection and instead return a "multi-command" indicator that the caller handles.

Option A is cleaner because it keeps the split logic at the top level.

IMPORTANT: The original command string is passed to the worker unchanged. We only split for classification purposes. The worker executes the full "cargo fmt && cargo build" as-is.

RECURSIVE CLASSIFICATION: When classifying sub-commands, use the same classify_command() function. This means sub-commands go through all 5 tiers. Guard against infinite recursion by passing a depth parameter or a flag that prevents re-splitting.

File: rch-common/src/patterns.rs (classify_command at line 243, check_structure at line 515)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:08.232803684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T22:55:37.201663391Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qt9","depends_on_id":"bd-2myq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:33.907156013Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qt9","depends_on_id":"bd-9ka6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:08.240797956Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2mmt","title":"Audit Remediation: Hot-Path Performance & Correctness Fixes","description":"Epic covering 4 remaining items from the deep code audit of the RCH codebase (commit fbaeaab fixed the first 7).

These 4 workstreams address performance bottlenecks and correctness issues on the hot path (<5ms budget):
1. Blocking file I/O in async hook context (rch/src/hook.rs)
2. Static string allocations in classification (rch-common/src/patterns.rs)
3. RwLock overhead on small atomic types (rchd/src/workers.rs)
4. Multi-command string rejection blanket-rejects valid compilation chains (rch-common/src/patterns.rs)

Success criteria: All hot-path operations stay within budget, no blocking I/O in async contexts, zero unnecessary allocations on reject path, atomic operations replace locks where possible, and legitimate multi-command compilation chains are correctly classified.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:35:49.833697736Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T23:27:50.782522477Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":86,"issue_id":"bd-2mmt","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS (from AGENTS.md lines 250-258):

| Operation | Budget | Panic Threshold |
|-----------|--------|-----------------|
| Hook decision (non-compilation) | <1ms | 5ms |
| Hook decision (compilation) | <5ms | 10ms |
| Worker selection | <10ms | 50ms |
| Full pipeline | <15% overhead | 50% overhead |

ALL changes in this epic must preserve these budgets. The E2E test (bd-37v0) verifies this.

FAIL-OPEN PHILOSOPHY (AGENTS.md line 259):
"If anything times out or errors, allow local execution rather than blocking."

WS4 (multi-command) adds the most risk here — the fail-open bead (bd-16t3) addresses this explicitly.","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:27:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2mrw","title":"Dependabot: Configure automerge for lumera_ai","description":"Add GitHub Actions workflow to auto-merge Dependabot minor/patch updates for lumera_ai repo.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T07:39:58.851007463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T06:41:16.314316894Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T06:41:16.314258495Z","close_reason":"Mis-scoped: targets other repos (charmed_rust/lumera_ai), not this RCH repo","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2mur","title":"Create TransferProgress for rsync visualization","description":"Create TransferProgress in rch-common/src/ui/progress/transfer.rs for file sync operations:
- Upload and download progress with direction indicator (↑/↓)
- File count and byte count progress (dual progress bars)
- Current file name display (truncated with ellipsis)
- Transfer speed (MB/s) with moving average smoothing
- ETA calculation based on recent speed
- Compression ratio indicator when using zstd

Technical requirements:
- Parse rsync --info=progress2 output for accurate stats
- Use rich_rust ProgressBar with custom completed/total styling
- Include mini-sparkline for speed history (last 10 samples)
- Handle rsync's bursty output gracefully
- Support --quiet flag to suppress progress entirely
- Clean up progress line on completion (don't leave artifacts)

Example during upload:
↑ Syncing to worker1  [████████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░] 58%
   247/425 files │ 45.2/78.1 MB │ 12.3 MB/s │ ETA 2.7s
   Current: src/very/long/path/to/some/module/file.rs...

Example on completion:
✓ Synced 425 files (78.1 MB) in 6.3s (12.4 MB/s avg, 3.2:1 compression)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:10:17.479118570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:31:06.391918114Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:31:06.391841349Z","close_reason":"Implemented TransferProgress UI component","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2mur","depends_on_id":"bd-37o8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2mur","depends_on_id":"bd-39mp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2myq","title":"WS4.1: Implement shell command splitter","description":"Create a split_shell_commands(cmd: &str) -> Vec<&str> function that splits on unquoted ;, &&, and || operators.

Rules:
- ;, &&, and || inside single quotes, double quotes, or backticks are NOT delimiters
- Nested quoting must be handled: "he said 'hello && bye'" has no delimiter
- Escaped quotes (\' \") do not toggle quote state
- Each returned segment is trimmed of whitespace
- Pipes (|) within a segment are NOT split (they are part of one command)
- Differentiate | from || : a single pipe is NOT a command separator

Implementation approach:
  Walk the string character by character tracking quote state (in_single, in_double, in_backtick).
  When outside quotes, check for &&, ||, or ; and split there.

Location: rch-common/src/patterns.rs (new public helper function)

Test cases:
- "cargo build" → ["cargo build"]
- "cargo fmt && cargo build" → ["cargo fmt", "cargo build"]
- "echo '&&' && cargo build" → ["echo '&&'", "cargo build"]
- "cd /tmp && make -j4 || echo fail" → ["cd /tmp", "make -j4", "echo fail"]
- "a ; b ; c" → ["a", "b", "c"]
- "echo 'hello;world' && make" → ["echo 'hello;world'", "make"]
- "cargo build 2>&1 | tee log" → ["cargo build 2>&1 | tee log"] (single segment, pipe preserved)

File: rch-common/src/patterns.rs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:06.510363959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T22:55:15.555592542Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2myq","depends_on_id":"bd-9ka6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:06.518856699Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2myq","title":"WS4.1: Implement shell command splitter","description":"Create a split_shell_commands(cmd: &str) -> Vec<&str> function that splits on unquoted ;, &&, and || operators.

Rules:
- ;, &&, and || inside single quotes, double quotes, or backticks are NOT delimiters
- Nested quoting must be handled: "he said 'hello && bye'" has no delimiter
- Escaped quotes (\' \") do not toggle quote state
- Each returned segment is trimmed of whitespace
- Pipes (|) within a segment are NOT split (they are part of one command)
- Differentiate | from || : a single pipe is NOT a command separator

Implementation approach:
  Walk the string character by character tracking quote state (in_single, in_double, in_backtick).
  When outside quotes, check for &&, ||, or ; and split there.

Location: rch-common/src/patterns.rs (new public helper function)

Test cases:
- "cargo build" → ["cargo build"]
- "cargo fmt && cargo build" → ["cargo fmt", "cargo build"]
- "echo '&&' && cargo build" → ["echo '&&'", "cargo build"]
- "cd /tmp && make -j4 || echo fail" → ["cd /tmp", "make -j4", "echo fail"]
- "a ; b ; c" → ["a", "b", "c"]
- "echo 'hello;world' && make" → ["echo 'hello;world'", "make"]
- "cargo build 2>&1 | tee log" → ["cargo build 2>&1 | tee log"] (single segment, pipe preserved)

 {"id":"bd-2o9y","title":"Unit Tests for Daemon Renderables (DaemonBanner, JobLifecycleLog, etc.)","description":"## Comprehensive Unit Tests for Daemon UI Components

Tests for Phase 3 daemon renderables.

### Test Categories

#### 1. DaemonBanner Tests (bd-toa3)
```rust
use rchd::ui::{DaemonBanner, BannerConfig};

#[test]
fn test_daemon_banner_renders_version() {
    let banner = DaemonBanner::new()
        .version("1.2.3");
    
    let output = banner.render_to_string();
    assert!(output.contains("1.2.3"));
}

#[test]
fn test_daemon_banner_shows_worker_count() {
    let banner = DaemonBanner::new()
        .workers(5);
    
    let output = banner.render_to_string();
    assert!(output.contains("5") || output.contains("five"));
}

#[test]
fn test_daemon_banner_respects_width() {
    let banner = DaemonBanner::new()
        .width(40);
    
    let output = banner.render_to_string();
    for line in output.lines() {
        assert!(line.len() <= 40, "Line exceeds width: {}", line);
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_daemon_banner_foreground_vs_background() {
    let fg_banner = DaemonBanner::new().mode(DaemonMode::Foreground);
    let bg_banner = DaemonBanner::new().mode(DaemonMode::Background);
    
    let fg_output = fg_banner.render_to_string();
    let bg_output = bg_banner.render_to_string();
    
    // Foreground should have more detail
    assert!(fg_output.len() >= bg_output.len());
}
```

#### 2. JobLifecycleLog Tests (bd-3ndq)
```rust
use rchd::ui::{JobLifecycleLog, JobEvent, JobPhase};

#[test]
fn test_job_lifecycle_new_job() {
    let log = JobLifecycleLog::new("job-123");
    let event = log.event(JobPhase::Queued);
    
    let output = event.render_to_string();
    assert!(output.contains("job-123"));
    assert!(output.contains("Queued") || output.contains("queued"));
}

#[test]
fn test_job_lifecycle_phase_transitions() {
    let log = JobLifecycleLog::new("job-456");
    
    for phase in [JobPhase::Queued, JobPhase::Compiling, JobPhase::Syncing, JobPhase::Done] {
        let event = log.event(phase);
        // Should not panic
        event.render_to_string();
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_job_lifecycle_includes_worker() {
    let log = JobLifecycleLog::new("job-789")
        .worker("fast-builder");
    
    let event = log.event(JobPhase::Compiling);
    let output = event.render_to_string();
    assert!(output.contains("fast-builder"));
}

#[test]
fn test_job_lifecycle_timing() {
    let log = JobLifecycleLog::new("job-abc")
        .started_at(Instant::now());
    
    std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100));
    
    let event = log.event(JobPhase::Done);
    let output = event.render_to_string();
    // Should contain elapsed time
    assert!(output.contains("ms") || output.contains("s"));
}
```

use rchd::ui::{WorkerStatusPanel, WorkerStatus};

fn test_worker_status_panel_empty_fleet() {

fn test_worker_status_panel_shows_all_workers() {

        WorkerStatus::new("worker-1", "healthy"),
        WorkerStatus::new("worker-2", "busy"),
        WorkerStatus::new("worker-3", "offline"),
    ];
    
    let panel = WorkerStatusPanel::new(workers);
    let output = panel.render_to_string();
    
    assert!(output.contains("worker-1"));
    assert!(output.contains("worker-2"));
    assert!(output.contains("worker-3"));
}

#[test]
fn test_worker_status_panel_health_indicators() {
    let workers = vec![
        WorkerStatus::new("healthy-worker", "healthy"),
        WorkerStatus::new("sick-worker", "unhealthy"),
    ];
    
    let panel = WorkerStatusPanel::new(workers);
    let output = panel.render_to_string();
    
    // Different statuses should have different indicators
    // (exact format depends on implementation)
}

#[test]
fn test_worker_status_panel_sorts_by_status() {
    // Unhealthy workers should appear first for visibility
    let workers = vec![
        WorkerStatus::new("healthy", "healthy"),
        WorkerStatus::new("unhealthy", "unhealthy"),
    ];
    
    let panel = WorkerStatusPanel::new(workers).sort_unhealthy_first();
    let lines: Vec<&str> = panel.render_to_string().lines().collect();
    
    // Unhealthy should appear before healthy
    let unhealthy_pos = lines.iter().position(|l| l.contains("unhealthy")).unwrap();
    let healthy_pos = lines.iter().position(|l| l.contains("healthy") && !l.contains("un")).unwrap();
    assert!(unhealthy_pos < healthy_pos);
}

use rchd::ui::{ShutdownSequence, ShutdownPhase};

#[test]
fn test_shutdown_sequence_phases() {

    for phase in [

}

    let forced = ShutdownSequence::new().forced();
    
    let g_output = graceful.phase(ShutdownPhase::Done).render_to_string();
    let f_output = forced.phase(ShutdownPhase::Done).render_to_string();
    
    // Messages should differ
    assert_ne!(g_output, f_output);
}

 {"id":"bd-2p00","title":"Phase 2: CLI Commands - Rich Output for Status & Worker Commands","description":"# Phase 2: CLI Commands - Rich Output for Status & Worker Commands

## Overview

Transform all rch CLI command output with rich_rust formatting. Each command must detect OutputContext and only emit rich formatting when appropriate.

## Design Principles

- stderr for human-friendly rich output
- stdout reserved for machine data when in non-interactive contexts
- --json flag output must remain unaffected
- Context-aware: no rich output in hook mode or when piped

## Key Deliverables

### 1. StatusTable for `rch status`

```rust
pub struct StatusTable {
    daemon_status: DaemonInfo,
    worker_summary: WorkerSummary,
    active_builds: Vec<ActiveBuild>,
    recent_metrics: Option<PerformanceMetrics>,
}
```

Displays:
- Daemon: running/stopped, uptime, PID
- Workers: healthy/degraded/offline counts
- Active: current builds with progress
- Metrics: recent build times, cache hit rate

### 2. WorkerTable for `rch workers list`

```rust
pub struct WorkerTable {
    workers: Vec<WorkerRow>,
    show_offline: bool,
    sort_by: WorkerSortField,
}

pub struct WorkerRow {
    name: String,
    status: WorkerStatus,      // Green dot, yellow dot, red X
    slots: String,             // "4/8" used/total
    speed_score: Option<f64>,  // "87.3" or "-"
    last_build: Option<String>, // "2m ago" or "never"
}
```

### 3. ProbeResult for `rch workers probe`

```rust
pub struct ProbeResult {
    worker: String,
    ssh_status: ProbeStatus,    // success/timeout/refused
    latency_ms: Option<u64>,
    toolchain: Option<String>,
    disk_free: Option<String>,
    error: Option<String>,
}
```

### 4. BenchmarkTable for `rch workers benchmark`

```rust
pub struct BenchmarkTable {
    workers: Vec<BenchmarkRow>,
    baseline: Option<String>,
}

pub struct BenchmarkRow {
    worker: String,
    build_time_ms: u64,
    relative_speed: f64,    // 1.0 = baseline
    speed_score: f64,       // Computed score
}
```

### 5. ConfigDisplay for `rch config show`

```rust
pub struct ConfigDisplay {
    source: ConfigSource,       // file path or "defaults"
    sections: Vec<ConfigSection>,
    warnings: Vec<ConfigWarning>,
}
```

## Technical Requirements

### Context Detection
Each command must check OutputContext before rendering:

```rust
fn execute_status(ctx: &OutputContext) -> Result<()> {
    let data = collect_status_data()?;

    if ctx.is_machine() {
        // JSON to stdout
        println!("{}", serde_json::to_string(&data)?);
    } else if ctx.is_rich() {
        // Rich table to stderr
        let table = StatusTable::from(&data);
        CONSOLE.print_rich(table.render())?;
    } else {
        // Plain text to stderr
        eprintln!("{}", data.to_plain_string());
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

### Table Styling
All tables use consistent styling:
- Unicode box-drawing characters (with ASCII fallback)
- Header row with bold text
- Status columns with colored indicators
- Right-aligned numeric columns
- Truncation for long text with "..." suffix

## Required Unit Tests

### Test File: `rch/src/ui/cli_tests.rs`

```rust
#[test]
fn test_status_table_renders_daemon_info() {
    let table = StatusTable::new(DaemonInfo {
        running: true,
        pid: Some(12345),
        uptime_secs: 3600,
    });
    let rendered = table.render_to_string();

    assert!(rendered.contains("running") || rendered.contains("Running"));
    assert!(rendered.contains("12345") || rendered.contains("PID"));
}

#[test]
fn test_status_table_shows_worker_counts() {
    let table = StatusTable::new_with_workers(
        WorkerSummary { healthy: 3, degraded: 1, offline: 2 }
    );
    let rendered = table.render_to_string();

    assert!(rendered.contains("3"));  // healthy count
    assert!(rendered.contains("1"));  // degraded count
}

#[test]
fn test_worker_table_sorts_by_status() {
    let mut table = WorkerTable::new(vec![
        WorkerRow::new("offline-1", WorkerStatus::Offline),
        WorkerRow::new("healthy-1", WorkerStatus::Healthy),
    ]);
    table.sort_by(WorkerSortField::Status);

    // Healthy should come first
    let rendered = table.render_to_string();
    let healthy_pos = rendered.find("healthy-1").unwrap();
    let offline_pos = rendered.find("offline-1").unwrap();
    assert!(healthy_pos < offline_pos);
}

#[test]
fn test_worker_table_hides_offline_by_default() {
    let table = WorkerTable::new_hide_offline(vec![
        WorkerRow::new("online", WorkerStatus::Healthy),
        WorkerRow::new("offline", WorkerStatus::Offline),
    ]);
    let rendered = table.render_to_string();

    assert!(rendered.contains("online"));
    assert!(!rendered.contains("offline"));
}

#[test]
fn test_probe_result_shows_latency() {
    let result = ProbeResult::success("worker-1", 45);
    let rendered = result.render_to_string();

    assert!(rendered.contains("45") || rendered.contains("ms"));
}

#[test]
fn test_probe_result_shows_error_on_failure() {
    let result = ProbeResult::failed("worker-1", "Connection refused");
    let rendered = result.render_to_string();

    assert!(rendered.contains("Connection refused") || rendered.contains("refused"));
}

#[test]
fn test_benchmark_table_shows_relative_speed() {
    let table = BenchmarkTable::new(vec![
        BenchmarkRow::new("fast", 1000, 2.0),
        BenchmarkRow::new("slow", 2000, 1.0),
    ]);
    let rendered = table.render_to_string();

    assert!(rendered.contains("2.0") || rendered.contains("2x"));
}

#[test]
fn test_config_display_shows_source_path() {
    let display = ConfigDisplay::new("/home/user/.config/rch/config.toml");
    let rendered = display.render_to_string();

    assert!(rendered.contains("config.toml"));
}

#[test]
fn test_config_display_shows_warnings() {
    let mut display = ConfigDisplay::new("/path/config.toml");
    display.add_warning("Deprecated option: legacy_mode");
    let rendered = display.render_to_string();

    assert!(rendered.contains("Deprecated") || rendered.contains("legacy_mode"));
}

#[test]
fn test_tables_respect_no_color() {
    let table = StatusTable::default();
    let rendered = table.render_plain();

    // Plain rendering should have no ANSI codes
    assert!(!rendered.contains("\x1b["));
}
```

### Minimum Test Count: 15 tests
- StatusTable: 3 tests
- WorkerTable: 3 tests
- ProbeResult: 2 tests
- BenchmarkTable: 2 tests
- ConfigDisplay: 2 tests
- Plain text fallback: 2 tests
- Context detection: 1 test

## E2E Test Script

### `scripts/e2e_cli_rich_output.sh`

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# E2E Test: CLI Rich Output Phase 2
# Tests that CLI commands produce correct rich output

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
LOG_FILE="/tmp/rch_e2e_cli_rich_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }
log_pass() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] PASS $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }
log_fail() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] FAIL $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; exit 1; }

log "Starting CLI Rich Output E2E tests"
log "Log file: $LOG_FILE"

cargo build -p rch --release 2>&1 | tail -5 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
RCH="$PROJECT_ROOT/target/release/rch"

# Test 1: rch status produces output
log "Test 1: rch status command"
STATUS_OUTPUT=$($RCH status 2>&1 || true)
if [ -z "$STATUS_OUTPUT" ]; then
    log_fail "rch status produced no output"
fi
log_pass "rch status produces output"

# Test 2: rch workers list shows table structure
log "Test 2: rch workers list table"
WORKERS_OUTPUT=$($RCH workers list 2>&1 || true)
# Should have table-like structure (either Unicode boxes or dashes)
if ! echo "$WORKERS_OUTPUT" | grep -qE "[-─┌┐└┘│├┤┬┴┼]|Name|Worker|Status"; then
    log_fail "Workers list doesn't look like a table"
fi
log_pass "rch workers list produces table"

# Test 3: --json flag produces valid JSON
log "Test 3: --json flag JSON output"
JSON_OUTPUT=$($RCH status --json 2>/dev/null || true)
if ! echo "$JSON_OUTPUT" | jq -e . >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_fail "--json output is not valid JSON"
fi
log_pass "--json produces valid JSON"

# Test 4: --json has no ANSI codes
log "Test 4: --json no ANSI codes"
if echo "$JSON_OUTPUT" | grep -qP '\x1b\['; then
    log_fail "ANSI codes found in --json output"
fi
log_pass "--json output is clean"

# Test 5: NO_COLOR disables rich formatting
log "Test 5: NO_COLOR environment variable"
PLAIN_OUTPUT=$(NO_COLOR=1 $RCH status 2>&1 || true)
if echo "$PLAIN_OUTPUT" | grep -qP '\x1b\['; then
    log_fail "ANSI codes present despite NO_COLOR"
fi
log_pass "NO_COLOR disables ANSI codes"

# Test 6: Piped output (non-TTY) is plain
log "Test 6: Piped output is plain"
PIPED_OUTPUT=$($RCH status 2>&1 | cat)
# When piped, should detect non-TTY and avoid rich formatting
# (This is harder to test since we're always piped in scripts)
log_pass "Piped output test (manual verification recommended)"

# Test 7: rch config show displays config
log "Test 7: rch config show"
CONFIG_OUTPUT=$($RCH config show 2>&1 || true)
if ! echo "$CONFIG_OUTPUT" | grep -qi "config\|workers\|socket"; then
    log_fail "Config show doesn't display configuration"
fi
log_pass "rch config show displays config"

log "========================================"
log "All CLI Rich Output E2E tests passed!"
log "Full log: $LOG_FILE"
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] StatusTable renders daemon status, worker summary, active builds
- [ ] WorkerTable renders fleet with health indicators and sorting
- [ ] ProbeResult shows SSH connectivity, latency, toolchain info
- [ ] BenchmarkTable shows worker performance comparison
- [ ] ConfigDisplay shows configuration with source and warnings
- [ ] All commands detect OutputContext correctly
- [ ] --json flag produces machine-readable output (unaffected by rich)
- [ ] NO_COLOR disables all ANSI codes
- [ ] Tables use consistent Unicode box styling with ASCII fallback
- [ ] Unit tests pass: 15+ tests

 {"id":"bd-2q3b","title":"Test: Circuit Breaker Trip & Recovery","description":"## Purpose
Test circuit breaker behavior with real workers - tripping on failures, staying open, and recovering when workers become healthy.

## MANDATORY Logging
This test MUST use TestLogger for all phases:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_circuit_trip","phase":"induce_failure","msg":"Inducing failures","data":{"worker":"css","failure_count":0}}
{"ts":"...","level":"WARN","test":"test_circuit_trip","phase":"circuit_state","msg":"Circuit state change","data":{"worker":"css","previous":"closed","new":"open","failures":5}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_circuit_recovery","phase":"probe","msg":"Half-open probe","data":{"worker":"css","probe_result":"success"}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_circuit_recovery","phase":"circuit_state","msg":"Circuit recovered","data":{"worker":"css","previous":"half_open","new":"closed"}}
```

## Test Cases

### Circuit Tripping
1. Consecutive failures trip circuit:
   - Cause N consecutive failures to worker
   - Verify: circuit transitions to OPEN
   - Verify: requests no longer sent to that worker
   - Log: failure count, state transitions

2. Half-open probing:
   - Wait for circuit timeout
   - Verify: circuit enters HALF_OPEN
   - Verify: probe request sent
   - Log: timeout, probe attempt

3. Recovery on success:
   - Fix worker, send probe
   - Verify: circuit closes on success
   - Verify: worker receives traffic again
   - Log: probe result, state change

### Circuit State Persistence
4. Daemon restart preserves state:
   - Trip circuit, restart daemon
   - Verify: circuit state preserved (or reasonably recovered)
   - Log: state before/after restart

5. Multi-worker circuits:
   - Trip circuit for one worker
   - Verify: other workers unaffected
   - Verify: traffic routed to healthy workers
   - Log: per-worker circuit state

### Error Counting
6. Transient errors do not trip:
   - Single error followed by success
   - Verify: circuit stays closed
   - Log: error count, reset

7. Timeout errors count:
   - Slow worker causes timeouts
   - Verify: timeouts count toward circuit trip
   - Log: timeout as failure

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Circuit breaker trips on real failures
- [ ] Half-open probing works
- [ ] Recovery on success
- [ ] Multi-worker isolation correct
- [ ] ALL state transitions logged with structured JSON","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:25:57.413169248Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:31:53.147449028Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T00:31:53.147194338Z","close_reason":"Merged into bd-23n3 (Error Handling & Recovery tests include circuit breaker).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2q3b","depends_on_id":"bd-23n3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2q3b","depends_on_id":"bd-ccqy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Technical requirements:

- Include worker selection reasoning when --debug-routing enabled
- Aggregate similar events (avoid 100 'worker healthy' messages)
- Support RCHD_WORKER_STATUS_INTERVAL for refresh frequency

Example state change:
[14:31:00] [WORKERS] worker2: BUSY → IDLE (job j-a3f2 complete)
[14:31:00] [WORKERS] ● 3/3 online │ load: 1.4 avg │ queue: 2 jobs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:07:52.970631623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:46:03.273876514Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:46:03.273802546Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3v54","depends_on_id":"bd-1z6p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v54","depends_on_id":"bd-3u68","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3v9b","title":"WS3.1: Replace speed_score RwLock with AtomicU64","description":"Convert speed_score: RwLock<f64> (line 25) to AtomicU64 using f64::to_bits/from_bits.

Replace:
  pub speed_score: RwLock<f64>,
With:
  pub speed_score: AtomicU64,  // stores f64 via to_bits/from_bits

Update accessor (line 160-162):
  pub fn get_speed_score(&self) -> f64 {
      f64::from_bits(self.speed_score.load(Ordering::Relaxed))
  }
  pub fn set_speed_score(&self, v: f64) {
      self.speed_score.store(v.to_bits(), Ordering::Relaxed);
  }

This removes the async requirement from get_speed_score() — it no longer needs .await.
Update all callers in selection.rs to remove .await on speed_score reads.

 {"id":"bd-4iov","title":"WS1: Eliminate Blocking File I/O in Async Hook","description":"PROBLEM: rch/src/hook.rs uses std::fs::read_to_string (line 886) and std::fs::write (line 906) in TimingHistory::load() and ::save(). These are called from synchronous functions record_build_timing() (line 948) and estimate_timing_for_build() (line 983). While the callers themselves are sync, they are invoked from the async hook pipeline, meaning they block the current tokio thread for the duration of disk I/O.

Additionally, save() uses std::fs::create_dir_all (line 901) and std::fs::rename (line 907).

WHY IT MATTERS: The hook is the critical path for every command invocation. When these sync I/O calls execute on a tokio worker thread, they block that thread from processing other async tasks. On slow disks or under I/O pressure, this can stall concurrent operations.

SOLUTION: Wrap the sync I/O calls in tokio::task::spawn_blocking(). This keeps the methods synchronous (minimal cascading changes) while offloading the blocking work to tokio's dedicated blocking thread pool.

FILES: rch/src/hook.rs (TimingHistory impl, lines 873-910, callers at 948, 983)
RISK: Low — spawn_blocking is a mechanical wrapper change.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:36:02.705249793Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T22:54:16.799356438Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4iov","depends_on_id":"bd-2mmt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:36:02.718993284Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":79,"issue_id":"bd-4iov","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BACKGROUND: The RCH hook runs as a PreToolUse handler in Claude Code. Every tool invocation triggers it. The async runtime (tokio) processes the hook decision. When std::fs calls block the runtime thread, ALL concurrent async operations stall — not just this one hook call. On a busy system with multiple concurrent tool invocations, this can cascade.

MEASUREMENT: std::fs::read_to_string on a cold file can take 50-500μs depending on disk. tokio::fs moves this to a blocking thread pool, keeping the async executor free. The latency doesn't decrease, but the concurrency impact disappears entirely.

VALIDATION: After conversion, run `cargo test` in the rch crate. No behavioral changes expected — only the execution context changes from blocking-inline to blocking-offloaded.","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:49Z"},{"id":83,"issue_id":"bd-4iov","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CORRECTION FROM REVIEW: The original bead claimed load/save were async fns. They are actually synchronous methods called from sync callers (record_build_timing, estimate_timing_for_build). However, these sync callers ARE invoked from the async hook pipeline — so the blocking still occurs on a tokio worker thread.

The fix is spawn_blocking, NOT converting to tokio::fs. spawn_blocking is simpler:
- No need to make TimingHistory methods async
- No need to propagate async up the call chain
- tokio's blocking thread pool handles the I/O
- If the blocking pool is full, it spawns new threads (up to 512 by default)

This is the standard tokio pattern for unavoidable sync I/O in async contexts.","created_at":"2026-01-29T22:54:16Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-54u8","title":"WS2.1: Convert pattern constants to static str slices","description":"Convert String fields in classification structs to avoid allocations on the reject hot path.

Key structs (rch-common/src/patterns.rs):
  Classification (line 80): reason: String → Cow<'static, str>
  ClassificationTier (line 101): name: String, reason: String → Cow<'static, str> each

Change constructor helpers:

  Classification::compilation(...) → same

Tier name constants: define as &'static str:
  const TIER_INSTANT_REJECT: &str = "instant_reject";

  const TIER_KEYWORD_FILTER: &str = "keyword_filter";

Reason constants for common reasons:
  const REASON_COMMAND_PRESENT: &str = "command present";
  etc.

This changes public API types (Classification.reason becomes Cow). Check all consumers.
